LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER KRISTA GILLETTE'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS. MATTER CALLED. THE COURT READS TWO EMAILS FROM KRISTA GILLETTE INTO THE RECORD. DISCUSSIONS ARE HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSIONS, THE PARTIES AGREE TO CONTINUE THE MATTER ONE ADDITONAL TIME. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO MAY 17, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 302. IF A RESOLUTION IS REACHED THE PARTIES ARE TO INFORM THE COURT BY EMAIL. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER KRISTA GILLETTE'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS. THE MATTER IS CALLED. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE PARTIES HAVE REACHED AN AGREEMENT AND THAT IT HAS RECEIVED THE PARTIES STIPULATION & ORDER. DISCUSSIONS ARE HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSIONS, THE COURT STATES THAT IT WILL SIGN THE STIPULATION AND ORDER STRIKING THE LANGUAGE THAT A "WRIT SHALL ISSUE" IN THE PROPOSED ORDER (AS REPORTED IN THE OFFICIAL TRANSCRIPT). MS. JOHNSON STATES THAT SHE WILL FILE THE EX PARTE PETITION REQUESTING THAT THE PETITION BE DISMISSED WHICH WILL INCLUDE THAT THE COURT RETAIN JURISDICTION PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6. THE STIPULATION AND ORDER, AS MODIFIED, IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Motion By Respondent City And County Of San Francisco For Entry Of Judgment Denying Writ For Petition Of Administrative Mandamus And Traditional Mandamus
Off Calendar Jun-30-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Krista Gillette (628.345.6789) Petitioner in propria persona.
Margaret (Dee Dee) Schroeder, Esq. (415.554.3843; deedee.schroeder@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for Respondent.

The court noted the matter was not timely contested. The court waives non-compliance with tentative rule and allows oral argument.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Denying Writ For Petition Of Administrative Mandamus And Traditional Mandamus.

The City and County of San Francisco's motion "for entry of judgment denying writ for petition of administrative mandamus and traditional mandamus" is granted.

On May 11, 2022, I held an extensive evidentiary hearing on the City's motion for enforcement of the settlement agreement governing this case. An order was filed May 13 and petitioner Krista Gillette was notified of it on May 16. Gillette's June 27, 2022 opposition to the City's now-pending motion seeks reconsideration of my May 13 order's factual findings. However, reconsideration would be both untimely and unavailing.

The time limit for a reconsideration pleading is 10 days after notice of the at-issue order. (CCP 1008.) Further, the pleading seeking reconsideration must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) None of that is present here.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar May-05-2022 Off Calendar

Motion By Respondent City And County Of San Francisco For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Apr-18-2022 Continued To May-11-22 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kathleen Lois Morgan, Esq. (415.994.1342; kathleenmorgan@pacbell.net) for Petitioner Krista Gillette via Zoom.
Krista Gillette (650.425.0867; kristagillet@gmail.com) Petitioner, also present in courtroom.
Margaret (DeeDee) Schroeder, Esq. (415.554.3843; deedee.schroeder@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for Respondent.

DISCUSSED RE: HEARING DATE FOR CCP 646.6 hearing. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion for Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement.

The City and County of San Francisco has moved for "enforcement of settlement agreement" in this dog bite writ case. Petitioner is Krista Gillette. The dog at issue is named Shepard.

A "stipulated settlement agreement and order" was signed in the case by the parties and Judge Schulman and was filed on May 17, 2021. On June 8, 2021, Judge Schulman filed an order "retaining jurisdiction pursuant to CCP 664.6 pursuant to stipulation." Judge Schulman's order further states that "the Court shall retain jurisdiction over the parties to enforce the settlement agreement even after dismissal of this matter."

Paragraph 13 of the settlement agreement states: "The parties agree that if Petitioner fails to abide by the terms of the settlement agreement that [sic] Shepard will be immediately seized by ACC [Animal Care and Control]."

Paragraph 13 of the settlement agreement also provides: "Petitioner will be allowed a hearing on the sole issue of whether Petitioner violated a term of the settlement agreement."

The above gives rise to four questions I asked counsel to brief: (1) as the City is moving for "enforcement of [the] settlement agreement," do the settlement agreement provisions apply to the pending motion? (2) if so, who is to hold the hearing? (3) what is the hearing's format to be? (4) what are the consequences if petitioner is found to have "violated a term of the settlement agreement"?

Counsels' briefs having now been considered, I rule as follows:
First, as the parties agree, the settlement agreement provisions apply to the pending motion.
Second, as the parties also agree, the CCP 646.6 hearing is to be held before me. As the settlement agreement provides, the hearing will be "on the sole issue of whether Petitioner violated a term of the settlement agreement."

Third, to forestall due process disputes, I will take evidence from live witnesses subject to cross-examination. Given the hearing's "sole issue" and narrow scope, pre-hearing discovery is not needed.

Fourth, if, after hearing, petitioner is found to have violated a term of the settlement agreement, the consequence is that "Shepherd will be immediately seized by ACC," as the agreement states.

CCP 646.6 hearing is set for May 11, 2022 at 1:30pm. Parties to meet and confer re: witnesses.

Ms. Schroeder to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar May-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Kathleen Morgan, Esq. of The Law Offices of Kathleen Morgan, 415.994.1342/kathleenmorgan@pacbell.net, for the Petitioner Krista Gillette;
Atty. Sheila Johnson, Esq. of the Office of the City Attorney, 415.554.4230/sheila.johnson@sfcityatty.org, for the Respondent City and County of San Francisco.

PETITIONER KRISTA GILLETTE'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.

The matter is called. The Court notes that the Petitioner Krista Gillette is present on ZOOM but did not state an appearance. The Court notes that the parties have reached an agreement and that it has received the parties stipulation & order.

Ms. Johnson states that they agree to the substance of the agreement, that the City is requesting that the writ be withdrawn, and that the language in the stipulation & order that states that "the writ shall issue" should either be removed or stricken. Ms. Johnson notes that she is taking responsibility for this issue noting that she just informed Ms. Morgan prior to court this morning. Ms. Morgan states that her client thought that they thought that they would be done with this today and that her client thought that she would get Shepherd today. DISCUSSIONS are held.

At the conclusion of discussions, Ms. Johnson consents to the Court striking the language in the proposed order. Ms. Morgan states that she has the authority if Shepherd is released today. The Court states that it will sign the stipulation and order striking the language that a "writ shall issue" in the proposed order (as reported in the official court reporter's transcript).

Ms. Johnson states that since this was an error on her part and that she will file the ex parte petition requesting that the petition be dismissed which will include that the Court retain jurisdiction pursuant to CCP 664.6.

The stipulation and order, as modified, is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
